generation_mode_record.draft_source: deterministic speculative-mode fixture, not a speculative decoder run
generation_mode_record.acceptance_predicate: candidate remains experimental unless baseline, quality, residual, fallback, and negative-control refs exist
generation_mode_record.proposed_output_accounting: two draft branches proposed in fixture accounting
generation_mode_record.accepted_output_accounting: one branch accepted after deterministic semantic checks
generation_mode_record.wall_clock_time: measured in local validation command; no fixed timing claim
generation_mode_record.quality_or_pass_result: pass: acceptance rate is recorded for fixture accounting and residual boundary is present
generation_mode_record.repair_or_fallback: fallback to autoregressive reference if verifier rejects speculative candidate
metric_definitions: useful_solution_per_second: accepted deterministic fixture per local command duration
quality: verifier accepts only if semantic accounting and fallback refs pass
residual: speculative acceptance is not model-quality evidence
non_claims: Does not promote any Appendix C support state.
Does not prove speculative decoding speed, acceptance quality, or runtime behavior.

resource_budget_record.value_hypothesis: A speculative generation mode can be retained as experimental only when quality, residuals, fallback, and baseline references are explicit.
displaced_costs: no real speculative decoder, no model call, no deployment path
resource_budget_record.quality_predicate: experimental retention requires baseline and negative-control evidence refs plus fallback behavior
safety_gates: fallback must be named for medium-risk fixture
resource_budget_record.escalation_rule: Escalate any speculative record that promotes, omits fallback, or treats latency as the sole metric.
residuals: Synthetic accounting only; no empirical model speedup.

non_claims: Does not promote any support state.
Does not prove speculative decoding performance.
